key_findings: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate (18/18) vs baseline 67% (12/18)
Skill consistently adds: ranked table with ALL 8 styles, cost comparison as decision axis
Baseline consistently misses: comprehensive style coverage (evaluates 3-4 not 8), cost analysis
Both correctly identify the right architecture recommendation
Skill adds structured output (architecture.md sections with rationale, tradeoffs, evolution path)
non_discriminating_assertions: correct-recommendation: Both with_skill and without_skill make sensible recommendations
architecture-md-updated: Both update architecture.md (though skill version is more structured)
skill_advantages: Evaluates ALL 8 styles systematically, not just obvious candidates
Cost as explicit decision axis ($ to $$$$$)
Red flag detection (premature distribution, resume-driven architecture)
Structured architecture.md output with rationale, tradeoffs accepted, evolution path
Qualifying context questions when scores are close
